key_insight: PG-KINN couples a Kolmogorov-Arnold Network trial space with a Petrov-Galerkin weak form to improve physics-informed PDE solving, especially for hard forward and inverse problems.
engineering_relevance: This offers a more accurate and better-conditioned alternative to MLP-based PINNs for computational mechanics, nonlinear PDEs, and parameter identification.
method: KAN is used as the trial function approximator with spline-based learnable activations.
An independent compactly supported polynomial test space enforces a Petrov-Galerkin weak residual.
Gauss-Legendre quadrature and integration by parts reduce derivative order and improve conditioning.
evidence_signal: The method is evaluated on benchmarks including crack singularities, stress concentration, Neo-Hookean hyperelasticity, inverse heterogeneous media identification, and complex geometries, where it outperforms MLP baselines and prior KAN-based formulations.
watch_item: Performance depends on careful construction of localized test functions and quadrature; weak-form setups may be more implementation-sensitive than standard residual PINNs.
paper_title: PG-KINN: A Physics-Informed Petrov-Galerkin Kolmogorov-Arnold Network for Solving Forward and Inverse PDEs
